summary: Decompiled three game-mode functions in gm_16F1, gm_1BA8, and gmregclear, with companion header signature updates. The PR body describes unlock-table lookup, CSS match-type cycling, and clear bonus calculation. An automated GALE01 report recorded the three targeted gm functions as 100% new matches, while also reporting unrelated-looking new matches/improvements and two .sdata2 regressions.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholder for fn_80170110 with C implementation for unlock-table lookup and dispatch.
Updated prototypes for fn_80170110 and fn_801701C0 from unknown-style declarations to concrete return/parameter types used by the decompiled body.
Replaced placeholder for gm_801BEDA8 with CSS match-type cycling implementation and VS mode data sync.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Match type cycling on CSS

key_files.role: Replaced placeholder for fn_8017F09C with clear bonus calculation logic using timer and eligibility checks.
Updated fn_8017F09C prototype from unknown return/parameters to s32 fn_8017F09C(void).

what_changed: fn_80170110 was decompiled as an unlock check that finds a table entry by kind, derives flags, checks a mask, and dispatches to pl_80039418 for kind < 0xD7 or fn_801701C0 otherwise.
gm_801BEDA8 was decompiled as CSS match_type cycling: pending_scene_change == 2 decrements with wrap to 0x17, otherwise increments with modulo 24, then copies css->data into VsModeData.
fn_8017F09C was decompiled as a clear bonus calculation: checks eligibility fields, multiplies timer_seconds by data->x108, clamps negative results to 0, and otherwise returns 0 when ineligible.
Headers were updated to reflect recovered signatures for fn_80170110, fn_801701C0, and fn_8017F09C.
Automated report listed fn_8017F09C, gm_801BEDA8, and fn_80170110 as 100.00% new matches in their gm units.
smart_moves: The PR paired decompiled bodies with header prototype cleanup, avoiding stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for recovered functions.
The CSS match-type function used typed structures CSSData and VsModeData rather than raw offset-only pointer arithmetic in the shown diff.
The clear-bonus function kept the nonnegative return behavior explicit by assigning product to ret and clamping ret to 0 when product is negative.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When decompiling a body proves a function signature, update the owning header in the same focused change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80170110 changed from int fn_80170110(UNK_T, int, int, u8) to int fn_80170110(void*, int, int, u8)
raw_diff: fn_801701C0 changed from UNK_RET fn_801701C0(UNK_PARAMS) to int fn_801701C0(void*, int, int)
raw_diff: fn_8017F09C changed from UNK_RET fn_8017F09C(UNK_PARAMS) to s32 fn_8017F09C(void)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small game-mode matching PRs can be organized as placeholder replacement plus minimal header surface changes, with verification tied to the touched symbols.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: GALE01 report lists three targeted gm items at 100.00%

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function names were retained for unrecovered semantic names, while local variables used short role names such as css, vs, data, p, p2, curr, kind, flags, product, and ret.
Existing typed struct names were used where available, including CSSData, VsModeData, MinorScene, lbl_8046B6A0_t, and lbl_80472D28_t.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use modulo for forward wrap when the domain size is fixed: css->match_type = (css->match_type + 1) % 24.
Use explicit backward wrap for decrementing a bounded selector: decrement when nonzero, otherwise assign 0x17.
A goto label was used in fn_80170110 to share mask/flag checking after either finding a table entry or hitting sentinel kind 0x29A.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80170110 while loop and check label

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Clear bonus logic preserved an intermediate product/ret shape before negative clamp, which may have been relevant to matching the emitted code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8017F09C product and ret locals

review_feedback.feedback: Automated GALE01 report showed matched code +880 bytes and listed the three targeted gm functions as 100.00% new matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: encounter issue_comment Report for GALE01

review_feedback.feedback: The same report also listed two regressions in unmatched .sdata2 items: main/melee/gm/gmregclear .sdata2 -15 and main/melee/it/items/itclimbersice .sdata2 -4.

follow_up_queries: fn_80170110 unlock table lbl_803D5A4C kind flags sentinel 0x29A
fn_8017F09C timer_seconds x108 clear bonus negative clamp
header prototype cleanup UNK_RET UNK_PARAMS gm_16F1 gmregclear

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovering a body should drive header prototype cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2324 replaced placeholders for three gm functions and updated owning headers where signatures became known, including removing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_8017F09C and refining fn_80170110/fn_801701C0 prototypes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Bounded selector wrap forms in CSS match-type cycling
accepted_candidate_records.text: gm_801BEDA8 matched CSS match-type cycling with explicit backward wrap to 0x17 and forward wrap using modulo 24 before syncing css->data to VsModeData.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Match type cycling on CSS

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate whether PR #2324 report regressions need ledger context
source_update_candidates.text: The automated GALE01 report for PR #2324 lists targeted gm function matches, but also reports .sdata2 regressions in main/melee/gm/gmregclear and main/melee/it/items/itclimbersice. The slice does not explain cause or acceptance, so this should only be considered for a verification/regression ledger if corroborated elsewhere.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful historical regression context, but evidence is insufficient to classify it as an accepted tradeoff or source fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's functional descriptions as canonical symbol names; the diff retains address-style names for the decompiled functions.
Do not infer reviewer intent or approval rationale; no review comments or review records are present.
Do not treat the unrelated new matches/improvements in the GALE01 report as changed files in this PR without further evidence from their diffs.
